ip_outcome_note: Card name matches. PokemonTCG.io confirms xy12-3 is Caterpie, National Pokedex no. 10, artist Ken Sugimori. Art — green segmented caterpillar with large eyes, yellow antenna, frontal pose on leafy ground — matches Caterpie's canonical design and the original Base Set illustration style. Verification unambiguous.

trivia_bullets.text: The XY Evolutions set (XY12) was released November 2, 2016, as a deliberate tribute to the original Pokemon Base Set (1998). The majority of its cards reproduce Base Set artwork with original Ken Sugimori flat illustration styling — including this Caterpie, which replicates the original Base Set no. 45 illustration. The set was released to coincide with the 20th anniversary of the Pokemon franchise. [PokemonTCG.io API: xy12-3; WebSearch: PokeBeach/Evolutions announcement]

Ken Sugimori is the art director and lead character designer at Game Freak who designed the original 151 Pokemon. His flat, clean illustration style — visible here — defined the aesthetic of the original TCG and the franchise's visual identity. The Evolutions set deliberately preserved his pre-digital rendering style rather than updating to modern painted techniques. [WebSearch: PokeBeach/Evolutions; PokemonTCG.io API]

Flavor text on this card: 'It is covered with a green skin. When it grows, it sheds the skin, covers itself with silk, and becomes a cocoon.' This text matches the original Base Set flavor text. Caterpie is National Pokedex no. 10 and evolves into Metapod, then Butterfree. [PokemonTCG.io API: xy12-3]

related_cards.name: Butterfree
related_cards.note: Final evolution — also in the Evolutions set with Base Set art
